@@ -1441,7 +1441,7 @@
 {"id":"br-3ubu.3","title":"Interface mode contract: reconcile ADR/spec language with Config InterfaceModeResolver behavior","description":"Problem: There is mild contract drift between docs/specs and code around the 'interface mode' concept (MCP vs CLI), including: (1) ADR/specs emphasizing binary-level separation and no runtime switching, (2) Config::from_env reading INTERFACE_MODE, and (3) InterfaceModeResolver tests asserting env precedence. Currently interface_mode is mostly diagnostic metadata, but drift here risks future bugs and operator confusion.\n\nDeliverables:\n- Decide the canonical contract for INTERFACE_MODE: test-only knob vs supported override vs remove entirely.\n- Align code + comments + ADR/spec wording to the chosen contract.\n- Add regression tests to ensure the MCP server binary cannot be accidentally turned into a CLI surface (and that denial UX remains deterministic).\n\nDefinition of done:\n- ADR/specs + code behavior match; no misleading comments remain; and the misconfig path is tested.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RedHarbor","created_at":"2026-02-08T23:08:35.203875494Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T23:38:46.866485369Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T23:38:46.866461104Z","close_reason":"Aligned interface_mode contract with ADR-001: removed INTERFACE_MODE runtime override; binary stamps mode; tests updated","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3ubu.3","depends_on_id":"br-3ubu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:56:07Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":915,"issue_id":"br-3ubu.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Reconciled interface-mode contract to match ADR-001/MIGRATION_GUIDE: mode is binary-level only (no INTERFACE_MODE runtime env var).

Code changes:
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-core/src/config.rs
  - Removed INTERFACE_MODE env parsing from Config::from_env().
  - Removed InterfaceModeResolver/ResolvedMode/ModeProvenance (unused + contradicted ADR-001 Invariant 3).
  - Updated InterfaceMode docs/comments to state there is intentionally no INTERFACE_MODE env var.
  - Kept InterfaceMode enum + basic unit tests; removed resolver/env-precedence tests.
  - bootstrap_summary now reports interface_mode source as default (binary stamps the value).

- crates/mcp-agent-mail/src/main.rs
  - Stamps config.interface_mode = InterfaceMode::Mcp directly (binary-level, per ADR-001).

- crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/context.rs
  - Stamps Config::from_env() results with InterfaceMode::Cli in both sync and async CLI contexts.

Validation:
- cargo fmt -p mcp-agent-mail-core -p mcp-agent-mail -p mcp-agent-mail-cli --check
- cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-core --all-targets
- cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-cli --all-targets
- cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail --all-targets
- cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-core
- cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-cli --test perf_security_regressions (includes SEC-4 env override bypass attempt)
","created_at":"2026-02-08T23:38:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-3ujus","title":"[cli] robot output lacks committed golden fixtures","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:00:54.943663172Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:25:09.024362717Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:25:09.023936347Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit"]}
 {"id":"br-3v6or","title":"T7.3: Message/Thread/Mailbox/Outbox/FileRes resource description parity","description":"Match the exact Python descriptions for message, thread, mailbox, outbox, and file_reservations resources.

resource://message/{message_id}: "Read a single message by id within a project. When to use: Fetch the canonical body/metadata for rendering in a client after list/search. Retrieve attachments and full details for a given message id. Common mistakes: Omitting project when a message id might exist in multiple projects."

resource://thread/{thread_id}: "List messages for a thread within a project. When to use: Present a conversation view for a given ticket/thread key. Export a thread for summarization or reporting."

resource://file_reservations/{slug}: "List file_reservations for a project, optionally filtering to active-only. Why this exists: File reservations communicate edit intent and reduce collisions across agents. Surfacing them helps humans review ongoing work and resolve contention."
Include the two JSON-RPC examples (active_only=true and active_only=false).

resource://mailbox/{agent}: "List recent messages in an agent's mailbox with lightweight Git commit context."

resource://mailbox-with-commits/{agent}: "List recent messages in an agent's mailbox with commit metadata including diff summaries."

resource://outbox/{agent}: "List messages sent by the agent, enriched with commit metadata for canonical files."","notes":"Updated message/{id}, thread/{id}, mailbox/{agent}, mailbox-with-commits/{agent}, outbox/{agent}, all 4 views (urgent-unread, ack-required, acks-stale, ack-overdue), file_reservations/{slug}, product/{key} descriptions to match Python docstrings exactly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-15T01:59:52.795393179Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T05:02:26.453965520Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T05:02:26.453882494Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["doc-parity"]}

 {"id":"br-3vwi","title":"[epic] AgentMailTUI v2: FrankentUI super-console + global mail intelligence","description":"## Background
`br-1m6a` and `br-10wc` shipped a strong first-generation FrankentUI console and 8-screen operations TUI. That closed the baseline gap versus plain logs, but operators are still blocked by major UX/feature parity gaps.

## Remaining Gap (why this epic exists)
The current Rust implementation is still short of the target in five critical ways:
1. Dashboard information density and composition are below FrankentUI showcase quality, especially on large terminals.
2. Layout behavior is not yet truly reactive/adaptive across tiny, normal, and ultra-wide terminal footprints.
3. Search UX is functional but not yet equivalent to showcase-grade exploratory workflows with rich filtering and preview ergonomics.
4. Full Python web UI externally observed functionality is not yet explicit, test-gated parity in this plan.
5. Static export workflows (GitHub Pages + Cloudflare Pages) are not yet first-class, conformance-tested deliverables.

## Goal
Build AgentMailTUI v2 + Web parity as a showcase-grade, operator-first control center that combines:
- global mail intelligence across projects,
- high-fidelity markdown-first reading,
- reactive FrankentUI dashboards/widgets,
- and complete Python web UI + static export parity.

## Product Outcomes
- Faster triage: operators can locate, read, and act on critical communication in seconds.
- Better situational awareness: high-signal metrics and anomaly widgets stay visible without wasting screen real estate.
- Better trust: deterministic behavior, explicit parity contracts, strong tests, and enforceable performance budgets.
- Better deployment flexibility: identical observed behavior across TUI, web UI, and static-export surfaces.

## Definition of Done
- Global search and exploration workflows are practical on multi-project production-scale datasets.
- Message/thread preview surfaces provide readable, safe, styled markdown in terminal and web.
- FrankentUI widgets materially improve insight density and actionability with responsive behavior.
- Python web UI functionality is matched in Rust with conformance evidence.
- Static export to GitHub Pages and Cloudflare Pages is production-usable and fully tested.
- Comprehensive unit + integration + snapshot + PTY + scripted E2E coverage exists for each major track.
- E2E/regression suites emit forensic logs/artifacts (transcripts, traces, metrics, visual diffs) sufficient for root-cause analysis.
- Perf budgets are enforced in CI with trend visibility and explicit release gates.
- Operator docs and rollout gates are complete.

## Notes for Future Contributors
This epic is intentionally self-contained: children include architecture decisions, implementation tracks, testing, perf, docs, rollout controls, and parity governance so future sessions never need to reconstruct intent from external plans.","acceptance_criteria":"## Success Criteria
- All child tracks under `br-3vwi` are completed with acceptance criteria satisfied.
- Cross-project search, rich markdown, reactive FrankentUI dashboarding, and advanced widgets are demonstrably delivered in operator workflows.
- Python web UI externally observed behavior is matched in Rust and verified by parity/conformance tests.
- Static export workflows for GitHub Pages and Cloudflare Pages are implemented and validated.
- Comprehensive unit/integration/snapshot/PTY/scripted-E2E coverage exists with artifact-rich logging.
- Performance, reliability, documentation, and rollout gates are met with linked evidence.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-09T20:47:37.123171032Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T19:40:32.154276250Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T19:40:32.154247876Z","close_reason":"All child beads under br-3vwi.* are closed; closing stale epic.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","frankentui","markdown","search","tui"],"comments":[{"id":916,"issue_id":"br-3vwi","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Idea-wizard distilled priorities for this epic:\n\nTop 5 (highest leverage):\n1) Global cross-project search cockpit with facets + previews\n2) In-terminal GFM rendering parity for message comprehension\n3) FrankentUI advanced widgets with actionable drill-down\n4) Analytics/anomaly layer for proactive operations\n5) Deterministic test/perf harness to keep complexity safe\n\nNext 10 complementary investments: unified inbox/outbox explorer, thread board, ack pressure board, command palette expansion, quick actions/macros, keymap customization, stress harness, docs/demo workflows, staged rollout governance, post-launch telemetry loop.\n\nThese priorities are encoded directly in track dependencies so future contributors can recover intent without external docs.","created_at":"2026-02-09T20:49:23Z"},{"id":917,"issue_id":"br-3vwi","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Plan-space revision pass completed: added explicit user-facing subtasks for saved-query workflows, attachment/reference exploration, and anomaly explanation UX; expanded testing into dedicated script suites with detailed forensic logging contracts; and tightened rollout gates to depend on performance/fault-injection evidence. No feature scope was removed.","created_at":"2026-02-09T20:59:14Z"},{"id":918,"issue_id":"br-3vwi","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Plan-space optimization pass (2026-02-09): Added explicit search-quality and reliability coverage without reducing scope. New beads: br-3vwi.2.5 (relevance benchmark corpus), br-3vwi.10.14 (security/redaction E2E), br-3vwi.10.15 (macro+preset deterministic replay E2E), br-3vwi.10.16 (cross-terminal compatibility matrix). Tightened dependencies so release/doc tracks are explicitly gated on these outcomes and updated existing E2E bead descriptions to include saved-query and macro/preset workflows.","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:14:31Z"},{"id":919,"issue_id":"br-3vwi","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Plan-space refinement pass (2026-02-09):

- Strengthened scope for global search parity (project/product scope + query dialect consistency across MCP tools and TUI) in br-3vwi.2.3, br-3vwi.4.4, br-3vwi.10.6, br-3vwi.10.8.
- Expanded markdown safety/parity expectations (hostile payload handling, sanitizer-policy parity, deterministic visual artifacts) in br-3vwi.3.3, br-3vwi.10.7, br-3vwi.10.14.
- Replaced generic perf acceptance criteria with explicit, measurable gates in br-3vwi.9.1/.9.2/.9.3.
- Rewired deterministic harness dependencies to reduce serial bottleneck while preserving replay guarantees:
  - removed br-3vwi.10.19 <- br-3vwi.10.3
  - added br-3vwi.10.3 <- br-3vwi.10.19
- Added supporting dependencies so docs and E2E remain aligned with security/parity obligations.

Validation after edits:
- br dep cycles --json => 0 cycles
- bv --robot-insights: br-3vwi.10.19 remains top bottleneck, but score dropped materially (~178.92 -> ~89.46), indicating improved parallelization without dropping feature coverage.","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:28:45Z"},{"id":920,"issue_id":"br-3vwi","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Refinement pass (2026-02-09, late):

- Replaced template-style acceptance criteria with explicit measurable gates on 26 beads spanning search, rendering, explorer, widgets, analytics, test suites, docs, and rollout governance.
- Every touched bead now encodes concrete unit/E2E expectations and detailed diagnostic artifact requirements.
- Dependency optimization: removed br-3vwi.10.15 <- br-3vwi.10.3 to reduce unnecessary serialism while preserving deterministic replay prerequisites.

Validation:
- br dep cycles --json => count 0
- bv --robot-insights => rank-1 critical path length reduced from 19 to 18.
- No remaining br-3vwi acceptance criteria contain placeholder wording patterns used by template criteria.

Intent:
- Keep planning deeply self-contained and implementation-ready.
- Preserve all requested functionality while tightening quality gates before coding starts.","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:49:31Z"},{"id":921,"issue_id":"br-3vwi","author":"Codex","text":"Plan-space overhaul (2026-02-10):

- Elevated Python web UI parity from implicit expectation to explicit, gated deliverable via new track `br-3vwi.13` + child beads `13.1`..`13.8`.
- Added deterministic static export scope (GitHub Pages + Cloudflare Pages) with implementation, deployment, security, and conformance testing beads.
- Strengthened quality net with new test beads under `br-3vwi.10`:
  - `10.20` browser E2E parity suite
  - `10.21` reactive layout visual-regression matrix
  - `10.22` static export conformance + GH/CF smoke suite
- Upgraded dashboard/reactive UX plan under `br-3vwi.6`:
  - `6.5` showcase-grade dashboard composition and panel budgets
  - `6.6` reactive panel layout engine for tiny→ultrawide terminals
- Rewired dependencies so web/export parity gates docs/release and test matrices (not optional side work).

Intent: no feature removal, only scope clarification and stronger execution/test gating to satisfy parity + quality requirements.","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:25:51Z"},{"id":922,"issue_id":"br-3vwi","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Plan-space refinement pass (2026-02-10, compatibility hardening):

- Added explicit startup/transport parity commitments to web/export track scope and acceptance (`br-3vwi.13`), including no-reconfig compatibility for clients pinned to `http://127.0.0.1:8765/mcp/`.
- Expanded parity-matrix contract (`br-3vwi.13.1`) to include init/auth/path/failure-mode transport rows, not only page-level web behaviors.
- Added new gated bead `br-3vwi.13.9` for MCP startup/handshake compatibility lock and wired it into tests/docs/release gates.
- Tightened reactive dashboard/layout acceptance criteria (`br-3vwi.6.5`, `br-3vwi.6.6`) to prevent wasted-space outcomes and preserve tiny-terminal usability.
- Strengthened test acceptance on `br-3vwi.10.20` and `br-3vwi.13.8` to require startup/transport traces and deterministic repro artifacts.

Validation:
- `br dep cycles --json` => count 0
- `bv --robot-triage`, `bv --robot-insights`, `bv --robot-alerts` rerun successfully
- No feature scope removed; only stronger parity/quality gating added.
","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:30:49Z"},{"id":923,"issue_id":"br-3vwi","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Canonicalization merge pass (2026-02-10): `br-134z*` -> `br-3vwi*`

Decision:
- `br-3vwi` is the canonical epic because it already includes broader scope (Python web parity, static export GH/CF pages, startup/transport compatibility on 8765, and comprehensive testing/perf/docs/release gates).
- `br-134z` is treated as an overlapping branch; unique requirements were merged into canonical beads before closure.

Merged feature mapping (no scope dropped):
- `br-134z.1*` Reactive layout foundation -> `br-3vwi.6.6`, `br-3vwi.6.5`, `br-3vwi.10.21`
- `br-134z.2*` Showcase dashboard panels -> `br-3vwi.6.5`, `br-3vwi.6.2`, `br-3vwi.7.*`
- `br-134z.3*` Search cockpit specifics -> `br-3vwi.4.1`..`4.4`, `br-3vwi.10.8`
- `br-134z.4*` Shared widget primitives -> `br-3vwi.6.1` + `6.2` (explicit canonical widget list)
- `br-134z.5*` Visual polish/a11y/toast/theme -> `br-3vwi.6.3` (+ strengthened acceptance)
- `br-134z.6*` GFM rendering/sanitization/highlighting -> `br-3vwi.3.*`, `br-3vwi.10.2`, `br-3vwi.10.7`
- `br-134z.7*` Agent/reservation visualization -> `br-3vwi.5.2`, `br-3vwi.5.3`, `br-3vwi.10.9`
- `br-134z.8*` SystemHealth gauges/perf timeline -> NEW `br-3vwi.7.5`
- `br-134z.9*` Thread timeline visualization -> `br-3vwi.5.2` + NEW `br-3vwi.5.5`
- `br-134z.10*` Timeline screen rail/inspector/filtering -> NEW `br-3vwi.5.5`
- `br-134z.11*` ToolMetrics screen -> NEW `br-3vwi.7.5`
- `br-134z.12*` Snapshot/widget-unit/resize E2E -> `br-3vwi.10`, `br-3vwi.10.9`, `br-3vwi.10.21`

Canonical additions created during merge:
- `br-3vwi.5.5` Implement Timeline Explorer screen with event rail, inspector, and typed filtering.
- `br-3vwi.7.5` Implement SystemHealth + ToolMetrics observability surfaces with heatmaps and drill-downs.

Canonical acceptance strengthened on:
- `br-3vwi.6.1`, `br-3vwi.6.2`, `br-3vwi.6.3`, `br-3vwi.6.5`
- `br-3vwi.5.2`, `br-3vwi.5.3`
- `br-3vwi.10`, `br-3vwi.10.9`, `br-3vwi.10.21`

Rationale:
- Prevents duplicate execution tracks and coordination churn.
- Preserves maximum functionality while reducing plan ambiguity.
- Keeps all implementation/testing/release obligations under one dependency graph.","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:13:49Z"},{"id":924,"issue_id":"br-3vwi","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Near-completion status (2026-02-12): Only 2 open children remain: br-3vwi.12 (Rollout governance) and br-3vwi.12.3 (Post-launch telemetry). All implementation tracks (1-11, 13-14) are CLOSED. This epic's delivered infrastructure (14-screen TUI, CommandPalette, NotificationQueue, FacetRail, event ring buffer, markdown rendering, FrankentUI widgets) now forms the foundation for br-2bbt (TUI V2 Showcase-Grade Upgrade) which enhances the delivered work with HintRanker, virtualized lists, native charts, modals, and webapp-parity features.","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:40:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-3vwi.1","title":"[track] TUI v2 product contract + information architecture","description":"## Purpose
Define an explicit V2 product contract and information architecture before deep implementation. This prevents piecemeal UI growth and ensures every track aligns to the same operator workflows and parity obligations.

## Scope
- Primary operator jobs-to-be-done (incident triage, inbox zero, ack SLA, conflict diagnosis, cross-project discovery).
- Screen IA and navigation map (entry points, drill-down paths, command-palette routes).
- Legacy Python mail-web parity map (routes, filters, actions, markdown behavior, exports) and FrankentUI demo capability map.
- Reactive layout principles for tiny/normal/ultra-wide terminals and corresponding density targets.
- Success metrics, non-goals, and explicit parity exit criteria.

## Deliverables
- A single contract document in-repo with screen-level acceptance criteria.
- Stable vocabulary for entities: project, agent, message, thread, reservation, metric, anomaly, export artifact.
- Parity matrix linking Python web behavior to Rust TUI/web/static-export behavior.
- Clear done criteria each implementation track can test against.

## Testability
- Contract-level snapshot/checklist tests (keybinding map, screen registry invariants, parity checklist completeness).
- Validation script that fails if any required parity row is unowned by a bead.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Product contract and IA artifacts are complete, reviewed, and referenced by downstream tracks.
- Operator workflows and non-goals are explicit, including responsive design rules for different terminal sizes.
- Parity matrix covers Python web UI features plus static export behavior and maps each row to implementation/test beads.
- Track outputs remove ambiguity for sequencing and review notes are attached in bead comments.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"CopperRobin","created_at":"2026-02-09T20:47:37.282351543Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T01:54:14.543329452Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T01:54:14.543309885Z","close_reason":"Completed: published V2 contract/IA + parity diff artifacts","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["planning","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3vwi.1","depends_on_id":"br-3vwi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:56:07Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":925,"issue_id":"br-3vwi.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implementation note: treat this track as a self-contained mini-epic. Keep acceptance criteria explicit, preserve deterministic behavior, and include unit + integration/PTY coverage where applicable. If scope expands, add child beads rather than hiding work in ad-hoc commits.","created_at":"2026-02-09T20:49:23Z"},{"id":926,"issue_id":"br-3vwi.1","author":"CopperRobin","text":"Drafted initial contract skeleton: docs/SPEC-tui-v2-product-contract.md (in progress). Next: integrate br-3vwi.1.1 canonical IA/navigation artifact + flesh parity/metrics sections.","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:25:41Z"},{"id":927,"issue_id":"br-3vwi.1","author":"CopperRobin","text":"Canonical V2 contract/IA doc is now docs/TUI_V2_CONTRACT.md (supersedes docs/SPEC-tui-v2-product-contract.md). I added a web-route scheme section under deep-linking to keep TUI↔web↔export link shapes stable.","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:32:28Z"},{"id":928,"issue_id":"br-3vwi.1","author":"CopperRobin","text":"Track output landed: canonical V2 contract + IA at docs/TUI_V2_CONTRACT.md (published) and parity/differentiation matrix at docs/TUI_V2_PARITY_DIFF.md. Child beads br-3vwi.1.1/.1.2/.1.3 are closed. Downstream implementation tracks should reference the contract and link evidence back into their beads.","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:54:09Z"}]}
